string 2:
Oh, I'm so glad you're here! Listen. This weird letter just
came in.
{next}Sounds creepy.
{next}Weird, how?
{end}Yeah, that can be scary.

Right?! That's why I got in touch, because I was so creeped
out.

I don't know if I can explain... Oh! Here. Let me just show
you!

It's been awful. Hey! I don't want sympathy! You busy? Come
back when you can hear me out!

Take a look at this. Seems like an ordinary letter, right?
But if I turn it over...

See here? It says it's from Kosuke Misono. That's Inori's
pop!

But why would he send a letter to his own office? Weird,
right?
{next}Maybe it has to do with a case?
{next}Maybe it's something he can't say directly?
{next}It could be a memento from his travels.
It may have been sent by someone pretending to be him...

Maybe. I imagine there are a lot of things Inori's pop can't
tell anyone right now.

Oh, sure. Who doesn't mail themselves letters as mementos?
Come on! There's no way!

Anyway, inside the envelope was the key to a coin locker in
Shinjuku Station.

But I'm not brave enough to open that locker on my own, so I
want you to come with me.
{next}Could it be a bicycle key?
{next}Could it be a coin locker key?
{next}Could it be the key to the office vault?

I dunno... That wouldn't explain this number. And did the
guy ever ride a bike?

Wait... I think that could be it! Good thinking! You're a
genius!

But then why put the combination on the key? Also, the
number of digits don't match.

Hang on a minute while I look into this. If I take a photo,
then do an image search...

Anyway, inside the envelope was the key to a coin locker in
Shinjuku Station.

But I don't have the courage to open that locker on my own.
Let's go check it out now!

So, the key goes to one of these lockers... There! That
one's got the same number!

Inori's pop must've put something in here. Okay... let's see
what's inside...
Huh? What's this?
{next}Some sort of machine part?
{next}Part of a Digimon?
{next}A time bomb?

Seems like it. At least, it looks mechanical, though I'm not
sure what it would go to.

Maybe. It seems like it's made of metal, but some Digimon do
have metal parts.

I doubt it. There's no timer, and opening the door would
make a better trigger for a bomb.

But if Inori's pop sent this to us even though we're
enemies, it must be important.

Wait a minute... I feel like I've seen something like this
before.

Where could that have been? For the life of me, I can't
remember...
{next}Think back. I know you can do it!
{next}How long ago was this?
{next}You're sure it wasn't in a dream?

Right. This has to be big. Come on, Hiroko! Channel your
streamer energy and think!
Let me think... I feel like it was way before I met you...
Come on! Stop teasing me! I know it wasn't a dream...

I've got it! It was back at the site of that big disaster!
At least, I'm pretty sure...

I forgot all about it until now, but... that has to be where
I saw this before!
{next}So, Inori's dad stole evidence?
{next}What big disaster?
{next}I'm glad you remembered.
Maybe... No, I bet he was keeping it hidden.

The unsolved Nishi-Shinjuku Mass Building Collapse.
I investigated the scene myself.
Hee hee. Thanks! I'd hate to think I was going senile at my age!

Way back when, a whole group of buildings in Nishi-Shinjuku
collapsed all at once.

It was so strange, a lot of people still see it as an
unsolved mystery, not an accident.

A sudden land subsidence was the official explanation,
but that never made much sense.
{next}It could have been a Digimon.
{next}And people just accepted the subsidence explanation?
{next}Some people believe everything they hear.
Right. At the time, it was unclear, but now that seems likely.

Well, there was no better explanation at the time,
so they sort of had to.

True. Few people question the government, after all.
And so the incident faded into obscurity.

So Inori's pop DID look into that incident. I thought he
might. After all, that's when...
Uh... Never mind.

I'd say this calls for a reinvestigation! I'll go on ahead.
See you in Nishi-Shinjuku!

This is it! This is the site of the Nishi-Shinjuku Mass
Building Collapse.

There are all new buildings here now, but, back then, this
place was in shambles.
{next}Could it have been caused by a gate opening?
{next}Could a clash between Digimon have caused it?
{next}Maybe it was caused by a meteorite impact.

Yes, it may have been a side effect of the Digimon and human
worlds joining together...

This WAS the site of a battle between Digimon. It could be
related.

That was one theory at the time, but with no crater or any
other evidence, it didn't last.
...What is this?!

AT LAST... AT LAST I HAVE FOUND IT... THE MISSING PART OF
ME!

That thing seems crazy strong! And doesn't it look like what
we found in that locker?
RETURN... MY MISSING PIECE... TO ME!

Uh-oh... Looks like we're in for a fight! But... do we even
stand a chance?

Are you actually going to fight it?! Sorry! All I can offer
is moral support. Good luck!
{player}!
Who said that?!
Oh! Look who it is!

We sensed an immense power and rushed to your aid! 
The Royal Knights' strength is yours!

Yet this location cannot accommodate us all. Who would you
have as your chief support?
{next}Omnimon.
{next}Gallantmon.
{next}Magnamon.
{end}Another Digimon.

As you wish! Now, witness how the Royal Knights fight as
one!

I, Gallantmon, am yours to command! My holy lance, Gram,
shall strike good and true!
Very well! My ferocious flaming fists are at your disposal!

You may have any one of your choosing from the Royal
Knights.
{next}Dynasmon.
{next}LordKnightmon.
{next}UlforceVeedramon.
{end}Yet another Digimon.
My lord! By my honor as a knight, I pledge you my sword!

You are wise to choose me to fight at your side! This is
sure to be a splendid battle!

Right... Leave this battle to me. Now is the time to show my
true strength!

You may have any one of your choosing from the Royal
Knights.
{next}Craniamon.
{next}Kentaurosmon.
{next}Leopardmon.
{end}Yet another Digimon still.

I could not ask for a better foe against which to test my
mettle!

You need but call my name and I shall appear! Let us dazzle
our foe with my speed!

You would rely upon my strategic mind? Then I shall guide
you down the path to victory!

You may have any one of your choosing from the Royal
Knights.
{next}Examon.
{next}Gankoomon.
{next}Jesmon.
{next}Alphamon.
{end}Let me think that through again.

None of the enemy attacks shall get past my massive frame. I
shall be your shield!

Perfect! I've been itching for some action! Now, lemme show
ya what I can do!

You seek MY aid? But my master... No. You chose me. I shall
fight with all I have!

Make no mistake. I take up arms not to save humanity, but
because I long for this battle!

This location cannot accommodate us all. Who would you have
as your chief support?

URK... I SHALL... RETURN! HOWEVER MANY TIMES IT TAKES... I
SHALL... BE... REBORN!
W-We won...?

But, you know, I don't think it's dead. It seems more
like... it's gone into rest mode.

What if it suddenly reactivates and goes on the attack
again?
{next}We'll defeat it as many times as it takes!
{next}Is this Digimon what caused the building collapse?
{next}We should take it apart right now.

That's... very reassuring. Knowing you, I'm sure you'll
follow through on that, too.

Maybe, but it's hard to say for sure. I guess that's going
to remain a mystery.

I'd love to, but look at that armor... I bet it's way
tougher than we humans could handle.

Anyway, it makes sense that Inori's pop would investigate
the disaster. It's how Yuu died.

Why do you think he sent us that part? Did he want us to get
his revenge?
{next}Yeah, maybe.
{next}Maybe he wanted us to know the truth.
{next}Maybe he just couldn't be bothered.
Well, if so, I hope we lived up to his expectations.

Maybe. After all, there are other unsolved mysteries Digimon
may have caused as well.

Haha. Yeah, maybe. He certainly seems busy. He might have
used us to do his dirty work.

Whatever his reasons, I still want to believe in the guy,
you know?

We may not see eye to eye right now, but surely we share a
desire to keep people safe.

Anyway, here's your compensation for helping me deal with
that weird letter. Thanks!
